symbols: Roman helmet and armor — military authority and the power to take or to spare
kneeling suppliant — vulnerability and dependence on the mercy of the powerful
grand columned hall — the magnificence of Roman power contrasted with moral virtue
captive woman presented to the general — the test of self-mastery in the moment of triumph
description: Inside a grand classical hall with soaring columns, arches, and coffered vaults, a Roman general in a red cloak and plumed helmet stands at the center, gesturing toward a young woman beside him. A man kneels before them in supplication, one hand extended in plea. Behind the general, a soldier carries a standard with a round shield. Through the great arched opening at right, a distant landscape and figures are visible. Ornate vessels and military trophies rest on the floor in the foreground. The scene is bathed in warm, golden light from the open archway.
